Title: Chloroplasts prevent precocious flowering through a GOLDEN2-LIKE–B-BOX DOMAIN PROTEIN module

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GARP family Myb-related transcription factor; GOLDEN2-LIKE
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chloroplast retrograde signaling-linked repression of flowering; regulation of chloroplast development gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates BBX14, BBX15, and BBX16 transcription via CCAATC cis-elements in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major repressive hub linking chloroplast retrograde signaling to the photoperiodic CO–FT module; loss of GLK1/2 causes early flowering with elevated FT/TSF
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Chloroplast status; chloroplast retrograde signaling; norflurazon-induced chloroplast damage reduces GLK1/2 transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous cues promoting developmental transition and flowering
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter active in leaf, including petiole, mesophyll, and vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in leaf, including petiole, mesophyll, and vasculature; functionally important in leaf vasculature under long-day conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GLK1 promoter; 35S constitutive promoter; SUC2 phloem/leaf vasculature-specific promoter; KNAT1 shoot apical meristem-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis; overexpression; tissue-specific artificial miRNA knockdown; qRT-PCR of FT/TSF; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; norflurazon treatment; genetic interaction with co and ft
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses flowering; promotes BBX14/15/16 expression and thereby lowers FT/TSF expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes early flowering and reduced chlorophyll/chloroplast function; overexpression delays flowering
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE189177; comparative use of public RNA-seq datasets GEO: GSE42545, GEO: GSE78969, SRA: SRP216916
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to delay precocious flowering and coordinate vegetative growth with reproductive transition, though manipulation may affect chloroplast development and photosynthetic traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with GLK2; glk1/2 early flowering is fully suppressed by co and ft/tsf mutations; authors conclude flowering effect is independent of sugar/T6P-SPL pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLK1 acts with GLK2 downstream of chloroplast retrograde signaling to repress flowering under long days. 2. GLK1 directly activates BBX14/15/16 by binding their promoters. 3. Loss of GLK1/2 elevates FT/TSF and causes early flowering, while overexpression delays flowering.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GARP family Myb-related transcription factor; GOLDEN2-LIKE
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chloroplast retrograde signaling-linked repression of flowering; regulation of chloroplast development gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates BBX14, BBX15, and BBX16 transcription via CCAATC cis-elements in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts with GLK1 as the main signaling hub connecting chloroplast function to repression of photoperiodic flowering
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Chloroplast status; chloroplast retrograde signaling; norflurazon-induced chloroplast damage reduces GLK1/2 transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous cues promoting developmental transition and flowering
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter active in leaf, including petiole, mesophyll, and vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in leaf, including petiole, mesophyll, and vasculature; functionally important in leaf vasculature under long-day conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GLK2 promoter; 35S constitutive promoter; SUC2 phloem/leaf vasculature-specific promoter; KNAT1 shoot apical meristem-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis; overexpression; tissue-specific artificial miRNA knockdown; qRT-PCR of FT/TSF; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; norflurazon treatment; genetic interaction with co and ft
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses flowering; promotes BBX14/15/16 expression and thereby lowers FT/TSF expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss with glk1 causes early flowering and reduced chlorophyll/chloroplast function; overexpression delays flowering
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE189177; comparative use of public RNA-seq datasets GEO: GSE42545, GEO: GSE78969, SRA: SRP216916
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune flowering time while coupling to chloroplast performance; caution due to pleiotropic effects on chloroplast development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLK2 acts redundantly with GLK1 to repress flowering under long days. 2. GLK2 directly activates BBX14/15/16 promoters. 3. Reduced GLK2 activity contributes to elevated FT/TSF and early flowering in chloroplast-defective contexts.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class III B-box zinc finger protein with B-box and CCT domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photoperiodic flowering repression downstream of chloroplast retrograde signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Physically interacts with CO and suppresses CO-mediated FT transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the GLK1/2–BBX14/15/16 repressor module that limits FT induction and prevents precocious flowering
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Direct transcriptional activation by GLK1/2; chloroplast retrograde signaling; norflurazon/lincomycin/chloroplast defects reduce expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous developmental signals associated with flowering timing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaf; expressed in leaf vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaf and leaf vasculature under long days
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay; ChIP-qPCR for GLK binding; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; overexpression; artificial miRNA knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly to repress FT expression and flowering; individual knockdown alone showed little effect, but combined knockdown with BBX15/16 caused early flowering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression delays flowering; single-gene perturbation has limited phenotype due to redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE189177; comparative use of public RNA-seq datasets GEO: GSE42545, GEO: GSE78969, SRA: SRP216916
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target with BBX15/16 to modulate flowering time; redundancy means single-gene editing may be insufficient
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct GLK target via promoter CCAATC motifs; B-box domain required for CO interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBX14 is a direct downstream target of GLK1/2. 2. BBX14 protein interacts with CO in the nucleus through its B-box domain. 3. Together with BBX15/16, it represses CO-driven FT activation and delays flowering.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class III B-box zinc finger protein with B-box and CCT domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photoperiodic flowering repression downstream of chloroplast retrograde signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Physically interacts with CO and suppresses CO-mediated FT transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the GLK1/2–BBX14/15/16 module restraining FT induction and flowering
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Direct transcriptional activation by GLK1/2; chloroplast retrograde signaling; norflurazon/lincomycin/chloroplast defects reduce expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous developmental signals associated with flowering timing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaf; expressed in leaf vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaf and leaf vasculature under long days
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay; ChIP-qPCR for GLK binding; promoter motif mutation/GUS assay; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; CRISPR-Cas12a knockout; overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly to repress FT expression and flowering; individual knockout alone showed little effect, but combined knockdown with BBX14/16 caused early flowering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression delays flowering; single-gene loss shows weak phenotype due to redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE189177; comparative use of public RNA-seq datasets GEO: GSE42545, GEO: GSE78969, SRA: SRP216916
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Combinatorial engineering target for flowering control; redundancy suggests multiplex editing or stacking is required
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct GLK target via promoter CCAATC motif; mutation of motif strongly reduced promoter activity; B-box domain required for CO interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBX15 is directly activated by GLK1/2 through promoter binding. 2. BBX15 interacts with CO in the nucleus and represses CO-mediated FT activation. 3. BBX15 functions redundantly with BBX14/16 to delay flowering and confer norflurazon-responsive repression.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class III B-box zinc finger protein with B-box and CCT domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photoperiodic flowering repression downstream of chloroplast retrograde signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Physically interacts with CO and suppresses CO binding and CO-mediated FT transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct effector of the GLK module; overexpression reduces CO enrichment at FT promoter and delays flowering
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Direct transcriptional activation by GLK1/2; chloroplast retrograde signaling; norflurazon/lincomycin/chloroplast defects reduce expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous developmental signals associated with flowering timing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaf; expressed in leaf vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaf and leaf vasculature under long days
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay; ChIP-qPCR for GLK binding and CO enrichment at FT promoter; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; CRISPR-Cas12a knockout; overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly to repress FT expression and flowering; overexpression reduces FT and delays flowering; combined BBX14/15/16 knockdown causes early flowering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression delays flowering; single-gene loss has weak effect because of redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE189177; comparative use of public RNA-seq datasets GEO: GSE42545, GEO: GSE78969, SRA: SRP216916
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning flowering time and chloroplast retrograde signaling outputs; multiplex approaches likely needed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct GLK target via promoter CCAATC motif; B-box domain required for CO interaction; BBX16 overexpression reduced CO occupancy at FT promoter by ~40%
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBX16 is a direct target of GLK1/2 in chloroplast retrograde signaling. 2. BBX16 interacts with CO in the nucleus and inhibits CO occupancy/activation of FT. 3. BBX16 contributes redundantly with BBX14/15 to repress flowering and mediate norflurazon responsiveness.
